[[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡大神宮]] [[ja:神楽坂若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市十日市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] [[ja:日出若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (加須市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (名古屋市中区)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] * [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine forks]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)-qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] {{Shrine | Name = Wakamiya Hachimangū | location = {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|29|41.96|N|133|32|37|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} | Deities = {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命}} (Emperor Ōjin)
{{ill|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命}} (Empress Jingū)
{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神}} (Ichikishimahime, Tagitsuhime, Takiribime)
{{ill|Ogame-no-Kami|ja|淤加美神}} (enshrined in an auxiliary hall) | Founded = Traditionally **1185 (Bunji 1), December 30** | Former ranking = **Former Prefectural Shrine (県社)** | Annual Festival = **Kanda Matsuri** (1st Saturday of April, lasting 3 days)
**Autumn Festival** (November 6) | Main Hall = **Three-bay nagare-zukuri (三間社流造)** | image = [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 06.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan**. It was historically classified as a **prefectural shrine (県社)** under the **modern shrine ranking system (近代社格制度)**. The shrine crest (神紋) is the **Maridomoe (まり巴)**. == Deities == * **Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)** – {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * **Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)** – {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * **{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神}}** – {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命}} * **Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)** – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the **Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)**, in **1185 (Bunji 1)**, after defeating the **Taira clan**, **Minamoto no Yoritomo** established **Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū** at the site of his grandfather **Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)** residence in **Kyoto** and dedicated the lands of **Agawa District, Tosa Province** as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, **Wakamiya Hachimangū** was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the **Sengoku period**, in **1560 (Eiroku 3)**, **Chōsokabe Motochika** established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his **first battle**, which ended in victory. From then on, he **prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign**. Under the **Yamauchi clan**, who became **daimyō of Tosa Domain** during the **Edo period**, the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's **eight official prayer shrines**. During the **Meiji period**, with the introduction of the **modern shrine ranking system**, the shrine was classified as a **village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)**, and later elevated to **prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).** Today, it serves as the **main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas**, including **Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama**. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * **Worship Hall (拝殿)** - The hall was rebuilt in a **"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)** style by **Chōsokabe Motochika** to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the **Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall** was designed in **"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)** style for victory reporting. - The **tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)** is known as the **"winning insect"** (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * **Torii (鳥居)** - Before departing for the **Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)**, Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his **battle standard became caught on the torii**, which some viewed as an **ill omen**. - Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a **good sign** that they would **conquer the enemy**, but suffered **a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa**, losing his **heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika**. - In anger, he **destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea**. The torii's **foundation resurfaced** in a **1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake**, leading to its reconstruction in **1870 (Meiji 3)**. - The current **reinforced concrete torii** was built in **1980 (Shōwa 55)**. * **Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)** - Unveiled in **1999 (Heisei 11)** for the **400th anniversary of Motochika’s death**. - The statue depicts Motochika gripping a **spear** and attempting to seize a **map of Shikoku**, which displays the region's **territorial divisions at the time of his first battle**. - Height: **~7 meters (including base)**, Spear length: **~6 meters**. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * **Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)** – **Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)**, or possibly **Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真)** * **Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)** – **Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)** * **Sanza Shrine (三座神社)** – **Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)** * **Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)** – **Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)** * **Hayato Shrine (隼人社)** – **Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)** == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the **"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)**, this **rice planting festival** involves young women (早乙女) covering men in **mud**, signifying **fertility and health**. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as **"Onabare" (おなばれ)**, a traditional **procession featuring mikoshi** and shrine followers in **historical attire**. == Access == * **Bus:** {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通}} Katsurahama Line – **5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]]

From then on, he **prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign**. During the **Meiji period**, with the introduction of the **modern shrine ranking system**, the shrine was classified as a **village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)**, and later elevated to **prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).** Today, it serves as the **main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas**, including **Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama**. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * **Worship Hall (拝殿)** - The hall was rebuilt in a **"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)** style by **Chōsokabe Motochika** to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the **Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall** was designed in **"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)** style for victory reporting. - The **tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)** is known as the **"winning insect"** (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * **Torii (鳥居)** - Before departing for the **Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)**, Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his **battle standard became caught on the torii**, which some viewed as an **ill omen**. - Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a **good sign** that they would **conquer the enemy**, but suffered **a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa**, losing his **heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika**. - In anger, he **destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea**. The torii's **foundation resurfaced** in a **1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake**, leading to its reconstruction in **1870 (Meiji 3)**. - The current **reinforced concrete torii** was built in **1980 (Shōwa 55)**. * **Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)** - Unveiled in **1999 (Heisei 11)** for the **400th anniversary of Motochika’s death**. - The statue depicts Motochika gripping a **spear** and attempting to seize a **map of Shikoku**, which displays the region's **territorial divisions at the time of his first battle**. - Height: **~7 meters (including base)**, Spear length: **~6 meters**. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * **Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)** – **Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)**, or possibly **Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真)** * **Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)** – **Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)** * **Sanza Shrine (三座神社)** – **Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)** * **Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)** – **Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)** * **Hayato Shrine (隼人社)** – **Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)** == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the **"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)**, this **rice planting festival** involves young women (早乙女) covering men in **mud**, signifying **fertility and health**. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as **"Onabare" (おなばれ)**, a traditional **procession featuring mikoshi** and shrine followers in **historical attire**. == Access == * **Bus:** {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通}} Katsurahama Line – **5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[Category:New]] [[ja:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]]

Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan**. It was historically classified as a **prefectural shrine (県社)** under the **modern shrine ranking system (近代社格制度)**. The shrine crest (神紋) is the **Maridomoe (まり巴)**. == Deities == * **Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)** – {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * **Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)** – {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * **{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神}}** – {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命}} * **Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)** – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the **Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)**, in **1185 (Bunji 1)**, after defeating the **Taira clan**, **Minamoto no Yoritomo** established **Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū** at the site of his grandfather **Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)** residence in **Kyoto** and dedicated the lands of **Agawa District, Tosa Province** as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, **Wakamiya Hachimangū** was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the **Sengoku period**, in **1560 (Eiroku 3)**, **Chōsokabe Motochika** established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his **first battle**, which ended in victory. From then on, he **prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign**. Under the **Yamauchi clan**, who became **daimyō of Tosa Domain** during the **Edo period**, the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's **eight official prayer shrines**. During the **Meiji period**, with the introduction of the **modern shrine ranking system**, the shrine was classified as a **village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)**, and later elevated to **prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).** Today, it serves as the **main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas**, including **Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama**. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * **Worship Hall (拝殿)** - The hall was rebuilt in a **"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)** style by **Chōsokabe Motochika** to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the **Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall** was designed in **"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)** style for victory reporting. - The **tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)** is known as the **"winning insect"** (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * **Torii (鳥居)** - Before departing for the **Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)**, Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his **battle standard became caught on the torii**, which some viewed as an **ill omen**. - Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a **good sign** that they would **conquer the enemy**, but suffered **a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa**, losing his **heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika**. - In anger, he **destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea**. The torii's **foundation resurfaced** in a **1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake**, leading to its reconstruction in **1870 (Meiji 3)**. - The current **reinforced concrete torii** was built in **1980 (Shōwa 55)**. * **Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)** - Unveiled in **1999 (Heisei 11)** for the **400th anniversary of Motochika’s death**. - The statue depicts Motochika gripping a **spear** and attempting to seize a **map of Shikoku**, which displays the region's **territorial divisions at the time of his first battle**. - Height: **~7 meters (including base)**, Spear length: **~6 meters**. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * **Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)** – **Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)**, or possibly **Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真)** * **Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)** – **Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)** * **Sanza Shrine (三座神社)** – **Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)** * **Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)** – **Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)** * **Hayato Shrine (隼人社)** – **Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)** == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the **"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)**, this **rice planting festival** involves young women (早乙女) covering men in **mud**, signifying **fertility and health**. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as **"Onabare" (おなばれ)**, a traditional **procession featuring mikoshi** and shrine followers in **historical attire**. == Access == * **Bus:** {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通}} Katsurahama Line – **5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]]

**Autumn Festival** (November 6) | Main Hall = **Three-bay nagare-zukuri (三間社流造)** | image = [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 06.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan**. It was historically classified as a **prefectural shrine (県社)** under the **modern shrine ranking system (近代社格制度)**. The shrine crest (神紋) is the **Maridomoe (まり巴)**. == Deities == * **Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)** – {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * **Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)** – {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * **{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神}}** – {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命}} * **Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)** – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the **Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)**, in **1185 (Bunji 1)**, after defeating the **Taira clan**, **Minamoto no Yoritomo** established **Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū** at the site of his grandfather **Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)** residence in **Kyoto** and dedicated the lands of **Agawa District, Tosa Province** as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, **Wakamiya Hachimangū** was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the **Sengoku period**, in **1560 (Eiroku 3)**, **Chōsokabe Motochika** established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his **first battle**, which ended in victory. From then on, he **prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign**. Under the **Yamauchi clan**, who became **daimyō of Tosa Domain** during the **Edo period**, the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's **eight official prayer shrines**. During the **Meiji period**, with the introduction of the **modern shrine ranking system**, the shrine was classified as a **village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)**, and later elevated to **prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).** Today, it serves as the **main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas**, including **Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama**. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * **Worship Hall (拝殿)** - The hall was rebuilt in a **"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)** style by **Chōsokabe Motochika** to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the **Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall** was designed in **"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)** style for victory reporting. - The **tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)** is known as the **"winning insect"** (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * **Torii (鳥居)** - Before departing for the **Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)**, Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his **battle standard became caught on the torii**, which some viewed as an **ill omen**. - Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a **good sign** that they would **conquer the enemy**, but suffered **a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa**, losing his **heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika**. - In anger, he **destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea**. The torii's **foundation resurfaced** in a **1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake**, leading to its reconstruction in **1870 (Meiji 3)**. - The current **reinforced concrete torii** was built in **1980 (Shōwa 55)**. * **Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)** - Unveiled in **1999 (Heisei 11)** for the **400th anniversary of Motochika’s death**. - The statue depicts Motochika gripping a **spear** and attempting to seize a **map of Shikoku**, which displays the region's **territorial divisions at the time of his first battle**. - Height: **~7 meters (including base)**, Spear length: **~6 meters**. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * **Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)** – **Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)**, or possibly **Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真)** * **Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)** – **Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)** * **Sanza Shrine (三座神社)** – **Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)** * **Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)** – **Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)** * **Hayato Shrine (隼人社)** – **Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)** == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the **"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)**, this **rice planting festival** involves young women (早乙女) covering men in **mud**, signifying **fertility and health**. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as **"Onabare" (おなばれ)**, a traditional **procession featuring mikoshi** and shrine followers in **historical attire**. == Access == * **Bus:** {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通}} Katsurahama Line – **5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:11]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "宗像三女神}}" to "宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}"
revision.text:
[[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡大神宮]] [[ja:神楽坂若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市十日市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] [[ja:日出若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (加須市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (名古屋市中区)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] * [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine forks]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)-qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] {{Shrine | Name = Wakamiya Hachimangū | location = {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|29|41.96|N|133|32|37|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} | Deities = {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命}} (Emperor Ōjin)
{{ill|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命}} (Empress Jingū)
{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Ichikishimahime, Tagitsuhime, Takiribime)
{{ill|Ogame-no-Kami|ja|淤加美神}} (enshrined in an auxiliary hall) | Founded = Traditionally **1185 (Bunji 1), December 30** | Former ranking = **Former Prefectural Shrine (県社)** | Annual Festival = **Kanda Matsuri** (1st Saturday of April, lasting 3 days)
**Autumn Festival** (November 6) | Main Hall = **Three-bay nagare-zukuri (三間社流造)** | image = [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 06.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan**. It was historically classified as a **prefectural shrine (県社)** under the **modern shrine ranking system (近代社格制度)**. The shrine crest (神紋) is the **Maridomoe (まり巴)**. == Deities == * **Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)** – {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * **Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)** – {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * **{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}** – {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命}} * **Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)** – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the **Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)**, in **1185 (Bunji 1)**, after defeating the **Taira clan**, **Minamoto no Yoritomo** established **Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū** at the site of his grandfather **Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)** residence in **Kyoto** and dedicated the lands of **Agawa District, Tosa Province** as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, **Wakamiya Hachimangū** was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the **Sengoku period**, in **1560 (Eiroku 3)**, **Chōsokabe Motochika** established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his **first battle**, which ended in victory. From then on, he **prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign**. Under the **Yamauchi clan**, who became **daimyō of Tosa Domain** during the **Edo period**, the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's **eight official prayer shrines**. During the **Meiji period**, with the introduction of the **modern shrine ranking system**, the shrine was classified as a **village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)**, and later elevated to **prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).** Today, it serves as the **main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas**, including **Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama**. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * **Worship Hall (拝殿)** - The hall was rebuilt in a **"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)** style by **Chōsokabe Motochika** to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the **Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall** was designed in **"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)** style for victory reporting. - The **tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)** is known as the **"winning insect"** (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * **Torii (鳥居)** - Before departing for the **Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)**, Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his **battle standard became caught on the torii**, which some viewed as an **ill omen**. - Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a **good sign** that they would **conquer the enemy**, but suffered **a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa**, losing his **heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika**. - In anger, he **destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea**. The torii's **foundation resurfaced** in a **1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake**, leading to its reconstruction in **1870 (Meiji 3)**. - The current **reinforced concrete torii** was built in **1980 (Shōwa 55)**. * **Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)** - Unveiled in **1999 (Heisei 11)** for the **400th anniversary of Motochika’s death**. - The statue depicts Motochika gripping a **spear** and attempting to seize a **map of Shikoku**, which displays the region's **territorial divisions at the time of his first battle**. - Height: **~7 meters (including base)**, Spear length: **~6 meters**. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * **Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)** – **Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)**, or possibly **Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真)** * **Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)** – **Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)** * **Sanza Shrine (三座神社)** – **Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)** * **Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)** – **Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)** * **Hayato Shrine (隼人社)** – **Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)** == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the **"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)**, this **rice planting festival** involves young women (早乙女) covering men in **mud**, signifying **fertility and health**. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as **"Onabare" (おなばれ)**, a traditional **procession featuring mikoshi** and shrine followers in **historical attire**. == Access == * **Bus:** {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通}} Katsurahama Line – **5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]]

In contrast, the **Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall** was designed in **"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)** style for victory reporting. - The **tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)** is known as the **"winning insect"** (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * **Torii (鳥居)** - Before departing for the **Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)**, Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his **battle standard became caught on the torii**, which some viewed as an **ill omen**. - Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a **good sign** that they would **conquer the enemy**, but suffered **a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa**, losing his **heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika**. - In anger, he **destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea**. The torii's **foundation resurfaced** in a **1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake**, leading to its reconstruction in **1870 (Meiji 3)**. - The current **reinforced concrete torii** was built in **1980 (Shōwa 55)**. * **Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)** - Unveiled in **1999 (Heisei 11)** for the **400th anniversary of Motochika’s death**. - The statue depicts Motochika gripping a **spear** and attempting to seize a **map of Shikoku**, which displays the region's **territorial divisions at the time of his first battle**. - Height: **~7 meters (including base)**, Spear length: **~6 meters**. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * **Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)** – **Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)**, or possibly **Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真)** * **Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)** – **Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)** * **Sanza Shrine (三座神社)** – **Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)** * **Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)** – **Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)** * **Hayato Shrine (隼人社)** – **Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)** == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the **"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)**, this **rice planting festival** involves young women (早乙女) covering men in **mud**, signifying **fertility and health**. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as **"Onabare" (おなばれ)**, a traditional **procession featuring mikoshi** and shrine followers in **historical attire**. == Access == * **Bus:** {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通}} Katsurahama Line – **5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan**. It was historically classified as a **prefectural shrine (県社)** under the **modern shrine ranking system (近代社格制度)**. The shrine crest (神紋) is the **Maridomoe (まり巴)**. == Deities == * **Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)** – {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * **Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)** – {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * **{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}** – {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} * **Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)** – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the **Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)**, in **1185 (Bunji 1)**, after defeating the **Taira clan**, **Minamoto no Yoritomo** established **Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū** at the site of his grandfather **Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)** residence in **Kyoto** and dedicated the lands of **Agawa District, Tosa Province** as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, **Wakamiya Hachimangū** was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the **Sengoku period**, in **1560 (Eiroku 3)**, **Chōsokabe Motochika** established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his **first battle**, which ended in victory. From then on, he **prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign**. Under the **Yamauchi clan**, who became **daimyō of Tosa Domain** during the **Edo period**, the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's **eight official prayer shrines**. During the **Meiji period**, with the introduction of the **modern shrine ranking system**, the shrine was classified as a **village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)**, and later elevated to **prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).** Today, it serves as the **main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas**, including **Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama**. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * **Worship Hall (拝殿)** - The hall was rebuilt in a **"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)** style by **Chōsokabe Motochika** to ensure victory in battle. However, his **battle standard became caught on the torii**, which some viewed as an **ill omen**. - Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a **good sign** that they would **conquer the enemy**, but suffered **a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa**, losing his **heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika**. - In anger, he **destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea**. The torii's **foundation resurfaced** in a **1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake**, leading to its reconstruction in **1870 (Meiji 3)**. - The current **reinforced concrete torii** was built in **1980 (Shōwa 55)**. * **Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)** - Unveiled in **1999 (Heisei 11)** for the **400th anniversary of Motochika’s death**. - The statue depicts Motochika gripping a **spear** and attempting to seize a **map of Shikoku**, which displays the region's **territorial divisions at the time of his first battle**. - Height: **~7 meters (including base)**, Spear length: [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡大神宮]] [[ja:神楽坂若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市十日市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] [[ja:日出若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (加須市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (名古屋市中区)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] * [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine forks]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] {{Shrine | Name = Wakamiya Hachimangū | location = {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|29|41.96|N|133|32|37|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} | Deities = {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命}} (Emperor Ōjin)
{{ill|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命}} (Empress Jingū)
{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Ichikishimahime, Tagitsuhime, Takiribime)
{{ill|Ogame-no-Kami|ja|淤加美神}} (enshrined in an auxiliary hall) | Founded = Traditionally **1185 (Bunji 1), December 30** | Former ranking = **Former Prefectural Shrine (県社)** | Annual Festival = **Kanda Matsuri** (1st Saturday of April, lasting 3 days)
**Autumn Festival** (November 6) | Main Hall = **Three-bay nagare-zukuri (三間社流造)** | image = [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 06.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan**. It was historically classified as a **prefectural shrine (県社)** under the **modern shrine ranking system (近代社格制度)**. The shrine crest (神紋) is the **Maridomoe (まり巴)**. == Deities == * **Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)** – {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * **Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)** – {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * **{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}** – {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} * **Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)** – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the **Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)**, in **1185 (Bunji 1)**, after defeating the **Taira clan**, **Minamoto no Yoritomo** established **Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū** at the site of his grandfather **Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)** residence in **Kyoto** and dedicated the lands of **Agawa District, Tosa Province** as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, **Wakamiya Hachimangū** was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the **Sengoku period**, in **1560 (Eiroku 3)**, **Chōsokabe Motochika** established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his **first battle**, which ended in victory. From then on, he **prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign**. Under the **Yamauchi clan**, who became **daimyō of Tosa Domain** during the **Edo period**, the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's **eight official prayer shrines**. During the **Meiji period**, with the introduction of the **modern shrine ranking system**, the shrine was classified as a **village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)**, and later elevated to **prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).** Today, it serves as the **main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas**, including **Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama**. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * **Worship Hall (拝殿)** - The hall was rebuilt in a **"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)** style by **Chōsokabe Motochika** to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the **Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall** was designed in **"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)** style for victory reporting. - The **tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)** is known as the **"winning insect"** (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * **Torii (鳥居)** - Before departing for the **Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)**, Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his **battle standard became caught on the torii**, which some viewed as an **ill omen**. - Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a **good sign** that they would **conquer the enemy**, but suffered **a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa**, losing his **heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika**. - In anger, he **destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea**. The torii's **foundation resurfaced** in a **1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake**, leading to its reconstruction in **1870 (Meiji 3)**. - The current **reinforced concrete torii** was built in **1980 (Shōwa 55)**. * **Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)** - Unveiled in **1999 (Heisei 11)** for the **400th anniversary of Motochika’s death**. - The statue depicts Motochika gripping a **spear** and attempting to seize a **map of Shikoku**, which displays the region's **territorial divisions at the time of his first battle**. - Height: **~7 meters (including base)**, Spear length: **~6 meters**. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * **Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)** – **Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)**, or possibly **Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真)** * **Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)** – **Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)** * **Sanza Shrine (三座神社)** – **Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)** * **Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)** – **Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)** * **Hayato Shrine (隼人社)** – **Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)** == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the **"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)**, this **rice planting festival** involves young women (早乙女) covering men in **mud**, signifying **fertility and health**. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as **"Onabare" (おなばれ)**, a traditional **procession featuring mikoshi** and shrine followers in **historical attire**. == Access == * **Bus:** {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通}} Katsurahama Line – **5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]]

55)**. * **Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)** - Unveiled in **1999 (Heisei 11)** for the **400th anniversary of Motochika’s death**. - The statue depicts Motochika gripping a **spear** and attempting to seize a **map of Shikoku**, which displays the region's **territorial divisions at the time of his first battle**. - Height: **~7 meters (including base)**, Spear length: **~6 meters**. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * **Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)** – **Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)**, or possibly **Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真)** * **Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)** – **Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)** * **Sanza Shrine (三座神社)** – **Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)** * **Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)** – **Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)** * **Hayato Shrine (隼人社)** – **Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)** == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the **"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)**, this **rice planting festival** involves young women (早乙女) covering men in **mud**, signifying **fertility and health**. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as **"Onabare" (おなばれ)**, a traditional **procession featuring mikoshi** and shrine followers in **historical attire**. == Access == * **Bus:** {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通}} Katsurahama Line – **5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:new pages]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡大神宮]] [[ja:神楽坂若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市十日市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] [[ja:日出若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (加須市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (名古屋市中区)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] * [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine forks]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] {{Shrine | Name = Wakamiya Hachimangū | location = {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|29|41.96|N|133|32|37|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} | Deities = {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命}} (Emperor Ōjin)
{{ill|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命}} (Empress Jingū)
{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Ichikishimahime, Tagitsuhime, Takiribime)
{{ill|Ogame-no-Kami|ja|淤加美神}} (enshrined in an auxiliary hall) | Founded = Traditionally **1185 (Bunji 1), December 30** | Former ranking = **Former Prefectural Shrine (県社)** | Annual Festival = **Kanda Matsuri** (1st Saturday of April, lasting 3 days)
**Autumn Festival** (November 6) | Main Hall = **Three-bay nagare-zukuri (三間社流造)** | image = [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 06.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan**. It was historically classified as a **prefectural shrine (県社)** under the **modern shrine ranking system (近代社格制度)**. The shrine crest (神紋) is the **Maridomoe (まり巴)**. == Deities == * **Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)** – {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * **Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)** – {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * **{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}** – {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} * **Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)** – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the **Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)**, in **1185 (Bunji 1)**, after defeating the **Taira clan**, **Minamoto no Yoritomo** established **Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū** at the site of his grandfather **Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)** residence in **Kyoto** and dedicated the lands of **Agawa District, Tosa Province** as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, **Wakamiya Hachimangū** was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the **Sengoku period**, in **1560 (Eiroku 3)**, **Chōsokabe Motochika** established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his **first battle**, which ended in victory. From then on, he **prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign**. Under the **Yamauchi clan**, who became **daimyō of Tosa Domain** during the **Edo period**, the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's **eight official prayer shrines**. During the **Meiji period**, with the introduction of the **modern shrine ranking system**, the shrine was classified as a **village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)**, and later elevated to **prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).** Today, it serves as the **main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas**, including **Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama**. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * **Worship Hall (拝殿)** - The hall was rebuilt in a **"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)** style by **Chōsokabe Motochika** to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the **Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall** was designed in **"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)** style for victory reporting. - The **tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)** is known as the **"winning insect"** (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * **Torii (鳥居)** - Before departing for the **Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)**, Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his **battle standard became caught on the torii**, which some viewed as an **ill omen**. - Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a **good sign** that they would **conquer the enemy**, but suffered **a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa**, losing his **heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika**. - In anger, he **destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea**. The torii's **foundation resurfaced** in a **1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake**, leading to its reconstruction in **1870 (Meiji 3)**. - The current **reinforced concrete torii** was built in **1980 (Shōwa 55)**. * **Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)** - Unveiled in **1999 (Heisei 11)** for the **400th anniversary of Motochika’s death**. - The statue depicts Motochika gripping a **spear** and attempting to seize a **map of Shikoku**, which displays the region's **territorial divisions at the time of his first battle**. - Height: **~7 meters (including base)**, Spear length: **~6 meters**. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * **Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)** – **Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)**, or possibly **Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真)** * **Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)** – **Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)** * **Sanza Shrine (三座神社)** – **Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)** * **Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)** – **Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)** * **Hayato Shrine (隼人社)** – **Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)** == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the **"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)**, this **rice planting festival** involves young women (早乙女) covering men in **mud**, signifying **fertility and health**. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as **"Onabare" (おなばれ)**, a traditional **procession featuring mikoshi** and shrine followers in **historical attire**. == Access == * **Bus:** {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通}} Katsurahama Line – **5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:new pages]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]]

{{ill|Ogame-no-Kami|ja|淤加美神}} (enshrined in an auxiliary hall) | Founded = Traditionally **1185 (Bunji 1), December 30** | Former ranking = **Former Prefectural Shrine (県社)** | Annual Festival = **Kanda Matsuri** (1st Saturday of April, lasting 3 days)
**Autumn Festival** (November 6) | Main Hall = **Three-bay nagare-zukuri (三間社流造)** | image = [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 06.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan**. It was historically classified as a **prefectural shrine (県社)** under the **modern shrine ranking system (近代社格制度)**. The shrine crest (神紋) is the **Maridomoe (まり巴)**. == Deities == * **Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)** – {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * **Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)** – {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * **{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}** – {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} * **Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)** – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the **Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)**, in **1185 (Bunji 1)**, after defeating the **Taira clan**, **Minamoto no Yoritomo** established **Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū** at the site of his grandfather **Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)** residence in **Kyoto** and dedicated the lands of **Agawa District, Tosa Province** as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, **Wakamiya Hachimangū** was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the **Sengoku period**, in **1560 (Eiroku 3)**, **Chōsokabe Motochika** established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his **first battle**, which ended in victory. From then on, he **prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign**. Under the **Yamauchi clan**, who became **daimyō of Tosa Domain** during the **Edo period**, the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's **eight official prayer shrines**. During the **Meiji period**, with the introduction of the **modern shrine ranking system**, the shrine was classified as a **village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)**, and later elevated to **prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).** Today, it serves as the **main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas**, including **Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama**. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * **Worship Hall (拝殿)** - The hall was rebuilt in a **"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)** style by **Chōsokabe Motochika** to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the **Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall** was designed in **"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)** style for victory reporting. - The **tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)** is known as the **"winning insect"** (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * **Torii (鳥居)** - Before departing for the **Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)**, Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his **battle standard became caught on the torii**, which some viewed as an **ill omen**. - Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a **good sign** that they would **conquer the enemy**, but suffered **a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa**, losing his **heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika**. - In anger, he **destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea**. The torii's **foundation resurfaced** in a **1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake**, leading to its reconstruction in **1870 (Meiji 3)**. - The current **reinforced concrete torii** was built in **1980 (Shōwa 55)**. * **Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)** - Unveiled in **1999 (Heisei 11)** for the **400th anniversary of Motochika’s death**. - The statue depicts Motochika gripping a **spear** and attempting to seize a **map of Shikoku**, which displays the region's **territorial divisions at the time of his first battle**. - Height: **~7 meters (including base)**, Spear length: **~6 meters**. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * **Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)** – **Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)**, or possibly **Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真)** * **Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)** – **Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)** * **Sanza Shrine (三座神社)** – **Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)** * **Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)** – **Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)** * **Hayato Shrine (隼人社)** – **Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)** == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the **"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)**, this **rice planting festival** involves young women (早乙女) covering men in **mud**, signifying **fertility and health**. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as **"Onabare" (おなばれ)**, a traditional **procession featuring mikoshi** and shrine followers in **historical attire**. == Access == * **Bus:** {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通}} Katsurahama Line – **5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:new pages]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]]

**Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)** * **Sanza Shrine (三座神社)** – **Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)** * **Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)** – **Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)** * **Hayato Shrine (隼人社)** – **Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)** == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the **"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)**, this **rice planting festival** involves young women (早乙女) covering men in **mud**, signifying **fertility and health**. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as **"Onabare" (おなばれ)**, a traditional **procession featuring mikoshi** and shrine followers in **historical attire**. == Access == * **Bus:** {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通}} Katsurahama Line – **5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:new pages]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]]

[[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡大神宮]] [[ja:神楽坂若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市十日市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] [[ja:日出若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (加須市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (名古屋市中区)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] * [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine forks]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]] [[category:qq close]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]] [[category:qq close]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] {{Shrine | Name = Wakamiya Hachimangū | location = {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|29|41.96|N|133|32|37|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} | Deities = {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命}} (Emperor Ōjin)
{{ill|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命}} (Empress Jingū)
{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Ichikishimahime, Tagitsuhime, Takiribime)
{{ill|Ogame-no-Kami|ja|淤加美神}} (enshrined in an auxiliary hall) | Founded = Traditionally **1185 (Bunji 1), December 30** | Former ranking = **Former Prefectural Shrine (県社)** | Annual Festival = **Kanda Matsuri** (1st Saturday of April, lasting 3 days)
**Autumn Festival** (November 6) | Main Hall = **Three-bay nagare-zukuri (三間社流造)** | image = [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 06.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan**. It was historically classified as a **prefectural shrine (県社)** under the **modern shrine ranking system (近代社格制度)**. The shrine crest (神紋) is the **Maridomoe (まり巴)**. == Deities == * **Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)** – {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * **Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)** – {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * **{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}** – {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} * **Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)** – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the **Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)**, in **1185 (Bunji 1)**, after defeating the **Taira clan**, **Minamoto no Yoritomo** established **Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū** at the site of his grandfather **Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)** residence in **Kyoto** and dedicated the lands of **Agawa District, Tosa Province** as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, **Wakamiya Hachimangū** was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the **Sengoku period**, in **1560 (Eiroku 3)**, **Chōsokabe Motochika** established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his **first battle**, which ended in victory. From then on, he **prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign**. Under the **Yamauchi clan**, who became **daimyō of Tosa Domain** during the **Edo period**, the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's **eight official prayer shrines**. During the **Meiji period**, with the introduction of the **modern shrine ranking system**, the shrine was classified as a **village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)**, and later elevated to **prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).** Today, it serves as the **main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas**, including **Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama**. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * **Worship Hall (拝殿)** - The hall was rebuilt in a **"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)** style by **Chōsokabe Motochika** to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the **Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall** was designed in **"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)** style for victory reporting. - The **tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)** is known as the **"winning insect"** (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * **Torii (鳥居)** - Before departing for the **Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)**, Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his **battle standard became caught on the torii**, which some viewed as an **ill omen**. - Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a **good sign** that they would **conquer the enemy**, but suffered **a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa**, losing his **heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika**. - In anger, he **destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea**. The torii's **foundation resurfaced** in a **1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake**, leading to its reconstruction in **1870 (Meiji 3)**. - The current **reinforced concrete torii** was built in **1980 (Shōwa 55)**. * **Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)** - Unveiled in **1999 (Heisei 11)** for the **400th anniversary of Motochika’s death**. - The statue depicts Motochika gripping a **spear** and attempting to seize a **map of Shikoku**, which displays the region's **territorial divisions at the time of his first battle**. - Height: **~7 meters (including base)**, Spear length: **~6 meters**. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * **Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)** – **Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)**, or possibly **Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真)** * **Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)** – **Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)** * **Sanza Shrine (三座神社)** – **Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)** * **Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)** – **Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)** * **Hayato Shrine (隼人社)** – **Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)** == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the **"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)**, this **rice planting festival** involves young women (早乙女) covering men in **mud**, signifying **fertility and health**. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as **"Onabare" (おなばれ)**, a traditional **procession featuring mikoshi** and shrine followers in **historical attire**. == Access == * **Bus:** {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通}} Katsurahama Line – **5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:new pages]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:akamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡大神宮]] [[ja:神楽坂若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市十日市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] [[ja:日出若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (加須市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (名古屋市中区)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] * [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine forks]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New uncategorized original categories]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New uncategorized original categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] {{Shrine | Name = Wakamiya Hachimangū | location = {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|29|41.96|N|133|32|37|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} | Deities = {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命}} (Emperor Ōjin)
{{ill|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命}} (Empress Jingū)
{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Ichikishimahime, Tagitsuhime, Takiribime)
{{ill|Ogame-no-Kami|ja|淤加美神}} (enshrined in an auxiliary hall) | Founded = Traditionally **1185 (Bunji 1), December 30** | Former ranking = **Former Prefectural Shrine (県社)** | Annual Festival = **Kanda Matsuri** (1st Saturday of April, lasting 3 days)
**Autumn Festival** (November 6) | Main Hall = **Three-bay nagare-zukuri (三間社流造)** | image = [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 06.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan**. It was historically classified as a **prefectural shrine (県社)** under the **modern shrine ranking system (近代社格制度)**. The shrine crest (神紋) is the **Maridomoe (まり巴)**. == Deities == * **Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)** – {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * **Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)** – {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * **{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}** – {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} * **Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)** – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the **Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)**, in **1185 (Bunji 1)**, after defeating the **Taira clan**, **Minamoto no Yoritomo** established **Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū** at the site of his grandfather **Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)** residence in **Kyoto** and dedicated the lands of **Agawa District, Tosa Province** as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, **Wakamiya Hachimangū** was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the **Sengoku period**, in **1560 (Eiroku 3)**, **Chōsokabe Motochika** established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his **first battle**, which ended in victory. From then on, he **prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign**. Under the **Yamauchi clan**, who became **daimyō of Tosa Domain** during the **Edo period**, the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's **eight official prayer shrines**. During the **Meiji period**, with the introduction of the **modern shrine ranking system**, the shrine was classified as a **village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)**, and later elevated to **prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).** Today, it serves as the **main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas**, including **Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama**. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * **Worship Hall (拝殿)** - The hall was rebuilt in a **"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)** style by **Chōsokabe Motochika** to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the **Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall** was designed in **"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)** style for victory reporting. - The **tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)** is known as the **"winning insect"** (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * **Torii (鳥居)** - Before departing for the **Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)**, Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his **battle standard became caught on the torii**, which some viewed as an **ill omen**. - Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a **good sign** that they would **conquer the enemy**, but suffered **a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa**, losing his **heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika**. - In anger, he **destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea**. The torii's **foundation resurfaced** in a **1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake**, leading to its reconstruction in **1870 (Meiji 3)**. - The current **reinforced concrete torii** was built in **1980 (Shōwa 55)**. * **Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)** - Unveiled in **1999 (Heisei 11)** for the **400th anniversary of Motochika’s death**. - The statue depicts Motochika gripping a **spear** and attempting to seize a **map of Shikoku**, which displays the region's **territorial divisions at the time of his first battle**. - Height: **~7 meters (including base)**, Spear length: **~6 meters**. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * **Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)** – **Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)**, or possibly **Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真)** * **Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)** – **Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)** * **Sanza Shrine (三座神社)** – **Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)** * **Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)** – **Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)** * **Hayato Shrine (隼人社)** – **Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)** == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the **"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)**, this **rice planting festival** involves young women (早乙女) covering men in **mud**, signifying **fertility and health**. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as **"Onabare" (おなばれ)**, a traditional **procession featuring mikoshi** and shrine followers in **historical attire**. == Access == * **Bus:** {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通}} Katsurahama Line – **5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:new pages]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:akamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]] [[Category:New]]
