Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan'''. It was historically classified as a '''prefectural shrine (県社)''' under the '''modern shrine ranking system (近代社格制度)'''. The shrine crest (神紋) is the '''Maridomoe (まり巴)'''. == Deities == * '''Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)''' – {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * '''Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)''' – {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * '''{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}''' – {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} * '''Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)''' – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the '''Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)''', in '''1185 (Bunji 1)''', after defeating the '''Taira clan''', '''Minamoto no Yoritomo''' established '''Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū''' at the site of his grandfather '''Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)''' residence in '''Kyoto''' and dedicated the lands of '''Agawa District, Tosa Province''' as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' - The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. - The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' - Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. - Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. - In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. - The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' - Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. - The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. - Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通}} During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' - The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. - The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' - Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. - Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. - In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. - The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' - Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. - The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. - Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通}} Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通}} Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡大神宮]] [[ja:神楽坂若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市十日市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] [[ja:日出若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (加須市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (名古屋市中区)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] * [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine forks]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] {{Shrine | Name = Wakamiya Hachimangū | location = {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|29|41.96|N|133|32|37|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} | Deities = {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命}} (Emperor Ōjin)
{{ill|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命}} (Empress Jingū)
{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Ichikishimahime, Tagitsuhime, Takiribime)
{{ill|Ogame-no-Kami|ja|淤加美神}} (enshrined in an auxiliary hall) | Founded = Traditionally '''1185 (Bunji 1), December 30''' | Former ranking = '''Former Prefectural Shrine (県社)''' | Annual Festival = '''Kanda Matsuri''' (1st Saturday of April, lasting 3 days)
'''Autumn Festival''' (November 6) | Main Hall = '''Three-bay nagare-zukuri (三間社流造)''' | image = [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 06.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan'''. It was historically classified as a '''prefectural shrine (県社)''' under the '''modern shrine ranking system (近代社格制度)'''. The shrine crest (神紋) is the '''Maridomoe (まり巴)'''. == Deities == * '''Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)''' – {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * '''Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)''' – {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * '''{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}''' – {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} * '''Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)''' – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the '''Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)''', in '''1185 (Bunji 1)''', after defeating the '''Taira clan''', '''Minamoto no Yoritomo''' established '''Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū''' at the site of his grandfather '''Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)''' residence in '''Kyoto''' and dedicated the lands of '''Agawa District, Tosa Province''' as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' - The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. - The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' - Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. - Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. - In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. - The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' - Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. - The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. - Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通}} Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{ill|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命}} (Empress Jingū)

{{ill|Ogame-no-Kami|ja|淤加美神}} (enshrined in an auxiliary hall) | Founded = Traditionally '''1185 (Bunji 1), December 30''' | Former ranking = '''Former Prefectural Shrine (県社)''' | Annual Festival = '''Kanda Matsuri''' (1st Saturday of April, lasting 3 days)
'''Autumn Festival''' (November 6) | Main Hall = '''Three-bay nagare-zukuri (三間社流造)''' | image = [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 06.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan'''. It was historically classified as a '''prefectural shrine (県社)''' under the '''modern shrine ranking system (近代社格制度)'''. The shrine crest (神紋) is the '''Maridomoe (まり巴)'''. == Deities == * '''Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)''' – {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * '''Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)''' – {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * '''{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}''' – {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} * '''Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)''' – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the '''Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)''', in '''1185 (Bunji 1)''', after defeating the '''Taira clan''', '''Minamoto no Yoritomo''' established '''Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū''' at the site of his grandfather '''Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)''' residence in '''Kyoto''' and dedicated the lands of '''Agawa District, Tosa Province''' as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' - The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. - The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' - Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. - Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. - In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. - The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' - Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. - The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. - Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通}} Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan'''. It was historically classified as a '''prefectural shrine (県社)''' under the '''modern shrine ranking system (近代社格制度)'''. The shrine crest (神紋) is the '''Maridomoe (まり巴)'''. == Deities == * '''Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)''' – {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * '''Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)''' – {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * '''{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}''' – {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} * '''Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)''' – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the '''Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)''', in '''1185 (Bunji 1)''', after defeating the '''Taira clan''', '''Minamoto no Yoritomo''' established '''Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū''' at the site of his grandfather '''Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)''' residence in '''Kyoto''' and dedicated the lands of '''Agawa District, Tosa Province''' as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' - The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. - The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' - Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. - Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. - In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. - The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' - Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. - The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. - Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通}} Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} (Kōchi)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. - Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. - In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' - Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. - The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. - Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通}} Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] revision.text:
[[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡大神宮]] [[ja:神楽坂若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市十日市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] [[ja:日出若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (加須市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (名古屋市中区)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] * [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine forks]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:11]] {{Shrine | Name = Wakamiya Hachimangū | location = {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|29|41.96|N|133|32|37|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} | Deities = {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命}} (Emperor Ōjin)
{{ill|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命}} (Empress Jingū)
{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}} (Ichikishimahime, Tagitsuhime, Takiribime)
{{ill|Ogame-no-Kami|ja|淤加美神}} (enshrined in an auxiliary hall) | Founded = Traditionally '''1185 (Bunji 1), December 30''' | Former ranking = '''Former Prefectural Shrine (県社)''' | Annual Festival = '''Kanda Matsuri''' (1st Saturday of April, lasting 3 days)
'''Autumn Festival''' (November 6) | Main Hall = '''Three-bay nagare-zukuri (三間社流造)''' | image = [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 06.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan'''. It was historically classified as a '''prefectural shrine (県社)''' under the '''modern shrine ranking system (近代社格制度)'''. The shrine crest (神紋) is the '''Maridomoe (まり巴)'''. == Deities == * '''Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)''' – {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * '''Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)''' – {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * '''{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}''' – {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} * '''Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)''' – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the '''Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)''', in '''1185 (Bunji 1)''', after defeating the '''Taira clan''', '''Minamoto no Yoritomo''' established '''Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū''' at the site of his grandfather '''Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)''' residence in '''Kyoto''' and dedicated the lands of '''Agawa District, Tosa Province''' as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' - The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. - The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' - Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. - Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. - In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. - The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' - Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. - The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. - Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通}} Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]][[Category:qq]]

{{ill|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命}} (Empress Jingū)

{{ill|Ogame-no-Kami|ja|淤加美神}} (enshrined in an auxiliary hall) | Founded = Traditionally '''1185 (Bunji 1), December 30''' | Former ranking = '''Former Prefectural Shrine (県社)''' | Annual Festival = '''Kanda Matsuri''' (1st Saturday of April, lasting 3 days)
'''Autumn Festival''' (November 6) | Main Hall = '''Three-bay nagare-zukuri (三間社流造)''' | image = [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 06.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan'''. It was historically classified as a '''prefectural shrine (県社)''' under the '''modern shrine ranking system (近代社格制度)'''. The shrine crest (神紋) is the '''Maridomoe (まり巴)'''. == Deities == * '''Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)''' – {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * '''Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)''' – {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * '''{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}''' – {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} * '''Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)''' – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the '''Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)''', in '''1185 (Bunji 1)''', after defeating the '''Taira clan''', '''Minamoto no Yoritomo''' established '''Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū''' at the site of his grandfather '''Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)''' residence in '''Kyoto''' and dedicated the lands of '''Agawa District, Tosa Province''' as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' - The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. - The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' - Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. - Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. - In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. - The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' - Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. - The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. - Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通}} Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan'''. It was historically classified as a '''prefectural shrine (県社)''' under the '''modern shrine ranking system (近代社格制度)'''. The shrine crest (神紋) is the '''Maridomoe (まり巴)'''. == Deities == * '''Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)''' – {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * '''Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)''' – {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * '''{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}''' – {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} * '''Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)''' – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the '''Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)''', in '''1185 (Bunji 1)''', after defeating the '''Taira clan''', '''Minamoto no Yoritomo''' established '''Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū''' at the site of his grandfather '''Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)''' residence in '''Kyoto''' and dedicated the lands of '''Agawa District, Tosa Province''' as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' ** The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. ** The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' ** Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. ** Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. ** In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. ** The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' ** Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. ** The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. ** Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通}} Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] revision.text:
* {{Shrine | Name = Wakamiya Hachimangū | location = {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|29|41.96|N|133|32|37|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} | Deities = {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|12=draft}} (Emperor Ōjin)
{{ill|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命|12=draft}} (Empress Jingū)
{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=draft}} (Ichikishimahime, Tagitsuhime, Takiribime)
{{ill|Ogame-no-Kami|ja|淤加美神|12=draft}} (enshrined in an auxiliary hall) | Founded = Traditionally '''1185 (Bunji 1), December 30''' | Former ranking = '''Former Prefectural Shrine (県社)''' | Annual Festival = '''Kanda Matsuri''' (1st Saturday of April, lasting 3 days)
'''Autumn Festival''' (November 6) | Main Hall = '''Three-bay nagare-zukuri (三間社流造)''' | image = [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 06.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan'''. It was historically classified as a '''prefectural shrine (県社)''' under the '''modern shrine ranking system (近代社格制度)'''. The shrine crest (神紋) is the '''Maridomoe (まり巴)'''. == Deities == * '''Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)''' – {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}} * '''Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)''' – {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}} * '''{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=draft}}''' – {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft}} * '''Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)''' – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the '''Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)''', in '''1185 (Bunji 1)''', after defeating the '''Taira clan''', '''Minamoto no Yoritomo''' established '''Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū''' at the site of his grandfather '''Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)''' residence in '''Kyoto''' and dedicated the lands of '''Agawa District, Tosa Province''' as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' ** The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. ** The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' ** Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. ** Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. ** In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. ** The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' ** Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. ** The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. ** Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通|12=draft}} Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine forks]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡大神宮]] [[ja:神楽坂若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市十日市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] [[ja:日出若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (加須市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (名古屋市中区)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]] [[sn:akamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)}}

Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通|12=draft}} Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine forks]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡大神宮]] [[ja:神楽坂若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市十日市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] [[ja:日出若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (加須市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (名古屋市中区)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]] [[sn:akamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]] revision.text:
* {{Shrine | Name = Wakamiya Hachimangū | location = {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|29|41.96|N|133|32|37|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} | Deities = {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|12=draft|12=draft}} (Emperor Ōjin)
{{ill|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命|12=draft|12=draft}} (Empress Jingū)
{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ichikishimahime, Tagitsuhime, Takiribime)
{{ill|Ogame-no-Kami|ja|淤加美神|12=draft|12=draft}} (enshrined in an auxiliary hall) | Founded = Traditionally '''1185 (Bunji 1), December 30''' | Former ranking = '''Former Prefectural Shrine (県社)''' | Annual Festival = '''Kanda Matsuri''' (1st Saturday of April, lasting 3 days)
'''Autumn Festival''' (November 6) | Main Hall = '''Three-bay nagare-zukuri (三間社流造)''' | image = [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 06.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan'''. It was historically classified as a '''prefectural shrine (県社)''' under the '''modern shrine ranking system (近代社格制度)'''. The shrine crest (神紋) is the '''Maridomoe (まり巴)'''. == Deities == * '''Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)''' – {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} * '''Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)''' – {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft|12=draft}} * '''{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=draft|12=draft}}''' – {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft|12=draft}} * '''Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)''' – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the '''Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)''', in '''1185 (Bunji 1)''', after defeating the '''Taira clan''', '''Minamoto no Yoritomo''' established '''Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū''' at the site of his grandfather '''Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)''' residence in '''Kyoto''' and dedicated the lands of '''Agawa District, Tosa Province''' as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' ** The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. ** The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' ** Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. ** Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. ** In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. ** The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' ** Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. ** The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. ** Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通|12=draft|12=draft}} Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine forks]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡大神宮]] [[ja:神楽坂若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市十日市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] [[ja:日出若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (加須市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (名古屋市中区)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]] [[sn:akamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)}}

Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' ** The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. ** The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' ** Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. ** Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. ** In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. ** The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' ** Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. ** The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. ** Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通|12=draft|12=draft}} Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine forks]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡大神宮]] [[ja:神楽坂若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市十日市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] [[ja:日出若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (加須市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (名古屋市中区)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]] [[sn:akamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)}}

* {{Shrine | Name = Wakamiya Hachimangū | location = {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|29|41.96|N|133|32|37|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} | Deities = {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|12=draft|12=draft}} (Emperor Ōjin)
{{ill|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命|12=draft|12=draft}} (Empress Jingū)
{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ichikishimahime, Tagitsuhime, Takiribime)
{{ill|Ogame-no-Kami|ja|淤加美神|12=draft|12=draft}} (enshrined in an auxiliary hall) | Founded = Traditionally '''1185 (Bunji 1), December 30''' | Former Ranking = '''Former Prefectural Shrine (県社)''' | Annual Festival = '''Kanda Matsuri''' (1st Saturday of April, lasting 3 days)
'''Autumn Festival''' (November 6) | Main Hall = '''Three-bay nagare-zukuri (三間社流造)''' | image = [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 06.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan'''. It was historically classified as a '''prefectural shrine (県社)''' under the '''modern shrine Ranking system (近代社格制度)'''. The shrine crest (神紋) is the '''Maridomoe (まり巴)'''. == Deities == * '''Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)''' – {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} * '''Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)''' – {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft|12=draft}} * '''{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=draft|12=draft}}''' – {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft|12=draft}} * '''Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)''' – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the '''Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)''', in '''1185 (Bunji 1)''', after defeating the '''Taira clan''', '''Minamoto no Yoritomo''' established '''Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū''' at the site of his grandfather '''Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)''' residence in '''Kyoto''' and dedicated the lands of '''Agawa District, Tosa Province''' as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' ** The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. ** The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' ** Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. ** Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. ** In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. ** The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' ** Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. ** The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. ** Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通|12=draft|12=draft}} Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine forks]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]]
