{{ill|Ogame-no-Kami|ja|淤加美神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (enshrined in an auxiliary hall) | Founded = Traditionally '''1185 (Bunji 1), December 30''' | Former Ranking = '''Former Prefectural Shrine (県社)''' | Annual Festival = '''Kanda Matsuri''' (1st Saturday of April, lasting 3 days)
'''Autumn Festival''' (November 6) | Main Hall = '''Three-bay nagare-zukuri (三間社流造)''' | image = [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 06.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan'''. It was historically classified as a '''prefectural shrine (県社)''' under the '''modern shrine Ranking system (近代社格制度)'''. The shrine crest (神紋) is the '''Maridomoe (まり巴)'''. == Deities == * '''Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)''' – {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)''' – {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' – {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)''' – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the '''Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)''', in '''1185 (Bunji 1)''', after defeating the '''Taira clan''', '''Minamoto no Yoritomo''' established '''Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū''' at the site of his grandfather '''Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)''' residence in '''Kyoto''' and dedicated the lands of '''Agawa District, Tosa Province''' as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' ** The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. ** The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' ** Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. ** Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. ** In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. ** The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' ** Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. ** The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. ** Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine forks]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡大神宮]] [[ja:神楽坂若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市十日市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] [[ja:日出若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (加須市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (名古屋市中区)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]] [[sn:akamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)}}

revision.comment: 34 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市) up to rev 103245943
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 若宮八幡宮 |画像 = [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 06.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[高知県]][[高知市]]長浜6600番地 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|29|41.96|N|133|32|37|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=若宮八幡宮}} |祭神 = [[応神天皇|誉田別命]]
[[神功皇后|息長帯比売命]]
[[宗像三女神]] |創建 = （伝）[[文治]]元年（[[1185年]]）[[12月30日]] |社格 = 旧[[県社]] |例祭 = 神田祭（4月第1土曜より3日）
秋季例祭（[[11月6日]]） |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]] }} '''若宮八幡宮'''（わかみやはちまんぐう）は、[[高知県]][[高知市]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。社紋は「まり巴」。 == 祭神 == * '''誉田別命''' （[[応神天皇]]） * '''息長帯比売命''' （[[神功皇后]]） * '''[[宗像三女神]]''' （[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]、[[タギツヒメ|湍津姫命]]、[[タキリビメ|田心姫命]]） * [[淤加美神|高靇神]] - 相殿に祭祀 == 歴史 == [[鎌倉時代]]の歴史書『[[吾妻鏡]]』の記述によれば、源頼朝は[[平氏]]を討ち果たした[[文治]]元年（[[1185年]]）に[[京都]]六条にあった祖父[[源為義]]邸跡に[[石清水八幡宮]]を勧請し六條若宮八幡宮を創建した。この時、[[土佐国]][[吾川郡]]一円を六條若宮八幡宮の神領とし、鎮護のために同年12月30日、当地にも若宮八幡宮を分祀したとされている。 [[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の[[永禄]]3年（[[1560年]]）[[長宗我部元親]]は[[初陣]]に臨み、若宮八幡宮馬場先に陣を構えた。この時に初陣を勝利で飾った。以来、元親は合戦のたびに当社で戦勝祈願を行うようになった。 江戸時代に入り[[山内氏]]が[[土佐藩]]主となって以後も崇敬され藩主祈願八社の一つとされた。 明治時代になり[[近代社格制度]]が制定されると、明治6年（[[1873年]]）郷社に列した。更に昭和5年（[[1930年]]）には県社となった。 現在は高知市南部（長浜、[[御畳瀬]]、[[浦戸 (高知市)|浦戸]]、[[横浜 (高知市)|横浜]]）の総鎮守となっている。 == 境内 == [[ファイル:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|元親が壊したとされる鳥居跡に建つ鳥居]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|長宗我部元親初陣像]] * 社殿 :: 長宗我部元親が合戦のたびに当社で戦勝祈願を行うようになり、拝殿は'''出蜻蛉式'''に立て直された。また、凱旋報告を行う[[土佐神社]]の拝殿を入蜻蛉式に改めた。日本では[[トンボ|蜻蛉]]は勝ち虫とも言われ古来より縁起物とされてきたことによる。 * 鳥居 :: [[天正]]14年（[[1586年]]）[[豊臣秀吉]]に従軍し[[島津氏|島津]]討伐に向かう際、元親は戦勝祈願を行った。この時、軍旗が[[鳥居]]の笠木に引っかかって落ちた。衆人はこれを不吉としたが、元親は「敵を笠にかけて討伐する吉兆である」と出陣した。しかし、[[戸次川の戦い]]で嫡子[[長宗我部信親|信親]]を失うなど惨敗となった。憤慨した元親は帰国後、この鳥居を不祥のものとして解体し海に流したという。[[幕末]]の[[慶応]]元年（[[1865年]]）に[[地震]]があった際に鳥居の基礎が浮き出たという。これは神意であると[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）木製にて鳥居を再建した。老朽化により[[昭和]]11年（[[1936年]]）に建て替えられた。現在の鳥居は、木製鳥居が老朽化したので昭和55年（[[1980年]]）鉄筋コンクリート製にて建て替えられたものである。 * 長宗我部元親初陣像 :: 外苑に建つ銅像。[[平成]]11年（[[1999年]]）元親没後400年を期して造られた。槍を持ち像の前にある[[四国]]地図を掴もうとする姿になっている。四国地図には初陣当時の四国の勢力分布が記されている。台座を含めた高さ約7[[メートル|m]]、槍の長さ約6mである。 == 摂末社 == * 天神宮 - 祭神：[[水分神|天水分大神]]。一説には[[菅原道真]]公 * 荒神宮（大荒神） - 祭神：大年御祖神、大土御祖神 * 三座神社 - 祭神：[[オオヤマツミ|山祇大神]]、[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂神]]、[[大国主|大国主神]] * 三社神社（椿社・薬神社） - 祭神：[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]]、[[ミズハノメ|水波能売神]]、[[スクナビコナ|少彦名神]] * 隼人社 - 祭神：福留隼人正 == 主な祭事 == === 神田祭 === [[ファイル:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|神田祭で使用される御神田]] 別名「[[どろんこ祭り]]」。[[田の神#早乙女|早乙女]]が[[男性]]に[[泥]]を塗る[[祭]]である。[[4月]]第1[[土曜日]]より3日間開かれる。 [[氏子]]を中心に約300名の男女が[[浴衣]]姿で集まり、[[早苗]]と白米をおにぎり状にした「かこ」と呼ばれる[[神饌]]を供えて[[豊作]]を祈願する。その後、氏子から選ばれた12名の早乙女により外苑の[[御田|御神田]]で[[田植え]]が行われる。田植えの後、早乙女が周囲にいる男性の[[顔]]や手足に泥を塗る。男性は逃げても構わないが捕まれば抵抗できないしきたりになっている。泥を塗られると無病で[[夏]]を乗り切れると言われている。 起源の伝承の一つとして、[[長宗我部氏]]の時代に疫病が流行った。若宮八幡宮に神田を奉納し疫病退散を祈願すると静まった。神田奉納の話は『長宗我部地検帳』に記載されている。
また、もう一つの挿話として、江戸時代になり土佐藩2代藩主[[山内忠義]]が領内視察を行った際に、早乙女の投げた早苗が泥をはね忠義の袴に掛かった。家臣が無礼討ちにしようとしたところ、忠義が「畦道は民百姓の道である。かような所を歩いていた我らの方が邪魔をした。今後一層仕事に励むように。」と許した。これを喜んだ人々が泥を掛け合って喜んだと言われている。 === 御神幸祭 === 別名「おなばれ」とも呼ばれる古式に則った御神幸式である。[[神輿]]とその前後に続く祭列が練り歩く。11月6日の秋季例祭で行われる。 行列は、お供衆、先祓い、神輿の順に進む。お供衆は、先頭を[[天狗]]面を被った[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦]]が進む。次に革籠が続く。革籠の中身は誰も確認したことがないが、言い伝えで[[源希義]]の遺品が入っているという。その後を土佐藩時代の風俗をした「お鉄砲組」が進む。先祓いとして、神輿の前を氏子から選ばれた10名の「練り子」と呼ばれる少年が白粉を塗り女装して木製の[[薙刀]]を持って舞いながら進む。神輿が神社に還御すると「行司ナバレ」という古式が社殿の前で奉納される。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[高知県]][[高知市]]長浜6600番地 '''交通アクセス''' * バス：[[とさでん交通]]桂浜線　南海中学校前停留所下車（南方向へ徒歩約5分） == 参考文献 == * 高知県高等学校教育研究会歴史部会 編 『高知県の歴史散歩』（山川出版社、[[2006年]]）71-72頁 * 『若宮八幡宮御由緒略記』 若宮八幡宮社務所 発行 * 現地説明板 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.city.kochi.kochi.jp/soshiki/39/wakamiyahachimanguu.html 若宮八幡宮]（高知市ホームページ） * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ 若宮八幡宮のホームページ] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わかみやはちまんくう こうちし}} [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:高知市の歴史]] [[Category:高知県の旧県社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:長宗我部氏|社わかみやはちまんくう こうちし]] [[Category:高知市の建築物]]

[[宗像三女神]] |創建 = （伝）[[文治]]元年（[[1185年]]）[[12月30日]] |社格 = 旧[[県社]] |例祭 = 神田祭（4月第1土曜より3日）
秋季例祭（[[11月6日]]） |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]] }} '''若宮八幡宮'''（わかみやはちまんぐう）は、[[高知県]][[高知市]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。社紋は「まり巴」。 == 祭神 == * '''誉田別命''' （[[応神天皇]]） * '''息長帯比売命''' （[[神功皇后]]） * '''[[宗像三女神]]''' （[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]、[[タギツヒメ|湍津姫命]]、[[タキリビメ|田心姫命]]） * [[淤加美神|高靇神]] - 相殿に祭祀 == 歴史 == [[鎌倉時代]]の歴史書『[[吾妻鏡]]』の記述によれば、源頼朝は[[平氏]]を討ち果たした[[文治]]元年（[[1185年]]）に[[京都]]六条にあった祖父[[源為義]]邸跡に[[石清水八幡宮]]を勧請し六條若宮八幡宮を創建した。この時、[[土佐国]][[吾川郡]]一円を六條若宮八幡宮の神領とし、鎮護のために同年12月30日、当地にも若宮八幡宮を分祀したとされている。 [[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の[[永禄]]3年（[[1560年]]）[[長宗我部元親]]は[[初陣]]に臨み、若宮八幡宮馬場先に陣を構えた。この時に初陣を勝利で飾った。以来、元親は合戦のたびに当社で戦勝祈願を行うようになった。 江戸時代に入り[[山内氏]]が[[土佐藩]]主となって以後も崇敬され藩主祈願八社の一つとされた。 明治時代になり[[近代社格制度]]が制定されると、明治6年（[[1873年]]）郷社に列した。更に昭和5年（[[1930年]]）には県社となった。 現在は高知市南部（長浜、[[御畳瀬]]、[[浦戸 (高知市)|浦戸]]、[[横浜 (高知市)|横浜]]）の総鎮守となっている。 == 境内 == [[ファイル:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|元親が壊したとされる鳥居跡に建つ鳥居]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|長宗我部元親初陣像]] * 社殿 :: 長宗我部元親が合戦のたびに当社で戦勝祈願を行うようになり、拝殿は'''出蜻蛉式'''に立て直された。また、凱旋報告を行う[[土佐神社]]の拝殿を入蜻蛉式に改めた。日本では[[トンボ|蜻蛉]]は勝ち虫とも言われ古来より縁起物とされてきたことによる。 * 鳥居 :: [[天正]]14年（[[1586年]]）[[豊臣秀吉]]に従軍し[[島津氏|島津]]討伐に向かう際、元親は戦勝祈願を行った。この時、軍旗が[[鳥居]]の笠木に引っかかって落ちた。衆人はこれを不吉としたが、元親は「敵を笠にかけて討伐する吉兆である」と出陣した。しかし、[[戸次川の戦い]]で嫡子[[長宗我部信親|信親]]を失うなど惨敗となった。憤慨した元親は帰国後、この鳥居を不祥のものとして解体し海に流したという。[[幕末]]の[[慶応]]元年（[[1865年]]）に[[地震]]があった際に鳥居の基礎が浮き出たという。これは神意であると[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）木製にて鳥居を再建した。老朽化により[[昭和]]11年（[[1936年]]）に建て替えられた。現在の鳥居は、木製鳥居が老朽化したので昭和55年（[[1980年]]）鉄筋コンクリート製にて建て替えられたものである。 * 長宗我部元親初陣像 :: 外苑に建つ銅像。[[平成]]11年（[[1999年]]）元親没後400年を期して造られた。槍を持ち像の前にある[[四国]]地図を掴もうとする姿になっている。四国地図には初陣当時の四国の勢力分布が記されている。台座を含めた高さ約7[[メートル|m]]、槍の長さ約6mである。 == 摂末社 == * 天神宮 - 祭神：[[水分神|天水分大神]]。一説には[[菅原道真]]公 * 荒神宮（大荒神） - 祭神：大年御祖神、大土御祖神 * 三座神社 - 祭神：[[オオヤマツミ|山祇大神]]、[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂神]]、[[大国主|大国主神]] * 三社神社（椿社・薬神社） - 祭神：[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]]、[[ミズハノメ|水波能売神]]、[[スクナビコナ|少彦名神]] * 隼人社 - 祭神：福留隼人正 == 主な祭事 == === 神田祭 === [[ファイル:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|神田祭で使用される御神田]] 別名「[[どろんこ祭り]]」。[[田の神#早乙女|早乙女]]が[[男性]]に[[泥]]を塗る[[祭]]である。[[4月]]第1[[土曜日]]より3日間開かれる。 [[氏子]]を中心に約300名の男女が[[浴衣]]姿で集まり、[[早苗]]と白米をおにぎり状にした「かこ」と呼ばれる[[神饌]]を供えて[[豊作]]を祈願する。その後、氏子から選ばれた12名の早乙女により外苑の[[御田|御神田]]で[[田植え]]が行われる。田植えの後、早乙女が周囲にいる男性の[[顔]]や手足に泥を塗る。男性は逃げても構わないが捕まれば抵抗できないしきたりになっている。泥を塗られると無病で[[夏]]を乗り切れると言われている。 起源の伝承の一つとして、[[長宗我部氏]]の時代に疫病が流行った。若宮八幡宮に神田を奉納し疫病退散を祈願すると静まった。神田奉納の話は『長宗我部地検帳』に記載されている。
また、もう一つの挿話として、江戸時代になり土佐藩2代藩主[[山内忠義]]が領内視察を行った際に、早乙女の投げた早苗が泥をはね忠義の袴に掛かった。家臣が無礼討ちにしようとしたところ、忠義が「畦道は民百姓の道である。かような所を歩いていた我らの方が邪魔をした。今後一層仕事に励むように。」と許した。これを喜んだ人々が泥を掛け合って喜んだと言われている。 === 御神幸祭 === 別名「おなばれ」とも呼ばれる古式に則った御神幸式である。[[神輿]]とその前後に続く祭列が練り歩く。11月6日の秋季例祭で行われる。 行列は、お供衆、先祓い、神輿の順に進む。お供衆は、先頭を[[天狗]]面を被った[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦]]が進む。次に革籠が続く。革籠の中身は誰も確認したことがないが、言い伝えで[[源希義]]の遺品が入っているという。その後を土佐藩時代の風俗をした「お鉄砲組」が進む。先祓いとして、神輿の前を氏子から選ばれた10名の「練り子」と呼ばれる少年が白粉を塗り女装して木製の[[薙刀]]を持って舞いながら進む。神輿が神社に還御すると「行司ナバレ」という古式が社殿の前で奉納される。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[高知県]][[高知市]]長浜6600番地 '''交通アクセス''' * バス：[[とさでん交通]]桂浜線　南海中学校前停留所下車（南方向へ徒歩約5分） == 参考文献 == * 高知県高等学校教育研究会歴史部会 編 『高知県の歴史散歩』（山川出版社、[[2006年]]）71-72頁 * 『若宮八幡宮御由緒略記』 若宮八幡宮社務所 発行 * 現地説明板 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.city.kochi.kochi.jp/soshiki/39/wakamiyahachimanguu.html 若宮八幡宮]（高知市ホームページ） * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ 若宮八幡宮のホームページ] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わかみやはちまんくう こうちし}} [[Category:高知県の神社]] [[Category:高知市の歴史]] [[Category:高知県の旧県社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:長宗我部氏|社わかみやはちまんくう こうちし]] [[Category:高知市の建築物]]

* {{Shrine | Name = Wakamiya Hachimangū | location = {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|29|41.96|N|133|32|37|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} | Deities = {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Emperor Ōjin)
{{ill|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Empress Jingū)
{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ichikishimahime, Tagitsuhime, Takiribime)
{{ill|Ogame-no-Kami|ja|淤加美神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (enshrined in an auxiliary hall) | Founded = Traditionally '''1185 (Bunji 1), December 30''' | Former Ranking = '''Former Prefectural Shrine (県社)''' | Annual Festival = '''Kanda Matsuri''' (1st Saturday of April, lasting 3 days)
'''Autumn Festival''' (November 6) | Main Hall = '''Three-bay nagare-zukuri (三間社流造)''' | image = [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 06.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan'''. It was historically classified as a '''prefectural shrine (県社)''' under the '''modern shrine Ranking system (近代社格制度)'''. The shrine crest (神紋) is the '''Maridomoe (まり巴)'''. == Deities == * '''Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)''' – {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)''' – {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' – {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)''' – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the '''Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)''', in '''1185 (Bunji 1)''', after defeating the '''Taira clan''', '''Minamoto no Yoritomo''' established '''Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū''' at the site of his grandfather '''Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)''' residence in '''Kyoto''' and dedicated the lands of '''Agawa District, Tosa Province''' as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' ** The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. ** The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' ** Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. ** Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. ** In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. ** The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' ** Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. ** The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. ** Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine forks]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡大神宮]] [[ja:神楽坂若宮八幡神社]] In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. ** The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' ** Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. ** Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. ** In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. ** The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' ** Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. ** The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. ** Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine forks]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]] '''Autumn Festival''' (November 6) | Main Hall = '''Three-bay nagare-zukuri (三間社流造)''' | image = [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 06.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan'''. It was historically classified as a '''prefectural shrine (県社)''' under the '''modern shrine Ranking system (近代社格制度)'''. The shrine crest (神紋) is the '''Maridomoe (まり巴)'''. == Deities == * '''Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)''' – {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)''' – {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)''' – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the '''Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)''', in '''1185 (Bunji 1)''', after defeating the '''Taira clan''', '''Minamoto no Yoritomo''' established '''Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū''' at the site of his grandfather '''Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)''' residence in '''Kyoto''' and dedicated the lands of '''Agawa District, Tosa Province''' as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' ** The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. ** The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' ** Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. ** Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. ** In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. ** The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' ** Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. ** The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. ** Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan'''. It was historically classified as a '''prefectural shrine (県社)''' under the '''modern shrine Ranking system (近代社格制度)'''. The shrine crest (神紋) is the '''Maridomoe (まり巴)'''. == Deities == * '''Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)''' – {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)''' – {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)''' – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the '''Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)''', in '''1185 (Bunji 1)''', after defeating the '''Taira clan''', '''Minamoto no Yoritomo''' established '''Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū''' at the site of his grandfather '''Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)''' residence in '''Kyoto''' and dedicated the lands of '''Agawa District, Tosa Province''' as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' ** The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. ** The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' ** Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. ** Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. ** In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. ** The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' ** Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. ** The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. ** Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡宮 (高知市)|version=103245943|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine forks]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] }} (大分市)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]] [[sn:akamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]]

revision.text:
* {{Shrine | Name = Wakamiya Hachimangū | location = {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōchi, Kōchi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōchi Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|29|41.96|N|133|32|37|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} | Deities = {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin)
{{ill|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Empress Jingū)
{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime, Tagitsuhime, Takiribime)
{{ill|Ogame-no-Kami|ja|淤加美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogame-no-Kami|qq=}} (enshrined in an auxiliary hall) | Founded = Traditionally '''1185 (Bunji 1), December 30''' | Former Ranking = '''Former Prefectural Shrine (県社)''' | Annual Festival = '''Kanda Matsuri''' (1st Saturday of April, lasting 3 days)
'''Autumn Festival''' (November 6) | Main Hall = '''Three-bay nagare-zukuri (三間社流造)''' | image = [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 06.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan'''. It was historically classified as a '''prefectural shrine (県社)''' under the '''modern shrine Ranking system (近代社格制度)'''. The shrine crest (神紋) is the '''Maridomoe (まり巴)'''. == Deities == * '''Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)''' – {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} * '''Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)''' – {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} * '''{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}''' – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} * '''Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)''' – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the '''Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)''', in '''1185 (Bunji 1)''', after defeating the '''Taira clan''', '''Minamoto no Yoritomo''' established '''Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū''' at the site of his grandfather '''Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)''' residence in '''Kyoto''' and dedicated the lands of '''Agawa District, Tosa Province''' as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' ** The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. ** The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' ** Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. ** Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. ** In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. ** The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' ** Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. ** The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. ** Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tosaden Kōtsū|qq=}} Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡宮 (高知市)|version=103245943|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine forks]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] }} [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡大神宮]] [[ja:神楽坂若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市十日市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] [[ja:日出若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (加須市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (名古屋市中区)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]] [[sn:akamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]]

Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan'''. It was historically classified as a '''prefectural shrine (県社)''' under the '''modern shrine Ranking system (近代社格制度)'''. The shrine crest (神紋) is the '''Maridomoe (まり巴)'''. == Deities == * '''Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)''' – {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} * '''Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)''' – {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} * '''{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}''' – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} * '''Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)''' – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the '''Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)''', in '''1185 (Bunji 1)''', after defeating the '''Taira clan''', '''Minamoto no Yoritomo''' established '''Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū''' at the site of his grandfather '''Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)''' residence in '''Kyoto''' and dedicated the lands of '''Agawa District, Tosa Province''' as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' ** The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. ** The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' ** Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. ** Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. ** In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. ** The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' ** Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. ** The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. ** Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' {{ill|Tosaden Kōtsū|ja|とさでん交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tosaden Kōtsū|qq=}} Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡宮 (高知市)|version=103245943|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] }} [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡大神宮]] [[ja:神楽坂若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市十日市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] [[ja:日出若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (加須市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (名古屋市中区)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]] [[sn:akamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]]

Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' [[:en:Tosaden Kōtsū|Tosaden Kōtsū]] Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] * {{Shrine | Name = Wakamiya Hachimangū | location = [[:en:Kōchi, Kōchi|Kōchi, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|29|41.96|N|133|32|37|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} | Deities = {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin)
{{ill|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Empress Jingū)
{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime, Tagitsuhime, Takiribime)
[[:en:Kuraokami|Ogame-no-Kami]] (enshrined in an auxiliary hall) | Founded = Traditionally '''1185 (Bunji 1), December 30''' | Former Ranking = '''Former Prefectural Shrine (県社)''' | Annual Festival = '''Kanda Matsuri''' (1st Saturday of April, lasting 3 days)
'''Autumn Festival''' (November 6) | Main Hall = '''Three-bay nagare-zukuri (三間社流造)''' | image = [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 06.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan'''. It was historically classified as a '''prefectural shrine (県社)''' under the '''modern shrine Ranking system (近代社格制度)'''. The shrine crest (神紋) is the '''Maridomoe (まり巴)'''. == Deities == * '''Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)''' – [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * '''Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)''' – [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * '''{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}''' – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} * '''Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)''' – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the '''Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)''', in '''1185 (Bunji 1)''', after defeating the '''Taira clan''', '''Minamoto no Yoritomo''' established '''Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū''' at the site of his grandfather '''Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)''' residence in '''Kyoto''' and dedicated the lands of '''Agawa District, Tosa Province''' as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' ** The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. ** The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' ** Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. ** Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. ** In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. ** The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' ** Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. ** The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. ** Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' [[:en:Tosaden Kōtsū|Tosaden Kōtsū]] Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan'''. It was historically classified as a '''prefectural shrine (県社)''' under the '''modern shrine Ranking system (近代社格制度)'''. The shrine crest (神紋) is the '''Maridomoe (まり巴)'''. == Deities == * '''Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)''' – [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * '''Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)''' – [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * '''{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}''' – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} * '''Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)''' – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the '''Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)''', in '''1185 (Bunji 1)''', after defeating the '''Taira clan''', '''Minamoto no Yoritomo''' established '''Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū''' at the site of his grandfather '''Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)''' residence in '''Kyoto''' and dedicated the lands of '''Agawa District, Tosa Province''' as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' ** The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. ** The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' ** Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. ** Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. ** In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. ** The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' ** Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. ** The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. ** Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' [[:en:Tosaden Kōtsū|Tosaden Kōtsū]] Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡宮 (高知市)|version=103245943|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] }} [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡大神宮]] [[ja:神楽坂若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市十日市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' [[:en:Tosaden Kōtsū|Tosaden Kōtsū]] Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] revision.text:
* {{Shrine | Name = Wakamiya Hachimangū | location = [[:en:Kōchi, Kōchi|Kōchi, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|29|41.96|N|133|32|37|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} | Deities = {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin)
{{ill|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Empress Jingū)
{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime, Tagitsuhime, Takiribime)
[[:en:Kuraokami|Ogame-no-Kami]] (enshrined in an auxiliary hall) | Founded = Traditionally '''1185 (Bunji 1), December 30''' | Former Ranking = '''Former Prefectural Shrine (県社)''' | Annual Festival = '''Kanda Matsuri''' (1st Saturday of April, lasting 3 days)
'''Autumn Festival''' (November 6) | Main Hall = '''Three-bay nagare-zukuri (三間社流造)''' | image = [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 06.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan'''. It was historically classified as a '''prefectural shrine (県社)''' under the '''modern shrine Ranking system (近代社格制度)'''. The shrine crest (神紋) is the '''Maridomoe (まり巴)'''. == Deities == * '''Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)''' – [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * '''Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)''' – [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * '''{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}''' – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} * '''Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)''' – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the '''Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)''', in '''1185 (Bunji 1)''', after defeating the '''Taira clan''', '''Minamoto no Yoritomo''' established '''Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū''' at the site of his grandfather '''Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)''' residence in '''Kyoto''' and dedicated the lands of '''Agawa District, Tosa Province''' as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' ** The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. ** The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' ** Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. ** Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. ** In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. ** The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' ** Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. ** The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. ** Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' [[:en:Tosaden Kōtsū|Tosaden Kōtsū]] Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan'''. It was historically classified as a '''prefectural shrine (県社)''' under the '''modern shrine Ranking system (近代社格制度)'''. The shrine crest (神紋) is the '''Maridomoe (まり巴)'''. == Deities == * '''Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)''' – [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * '''Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)''' – [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * '''{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}''' – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} * '''Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)''' – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the '''Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)''', in '''1185 (Bunji 1)''', after defeating the '''Taira clan''', '''Minamoto no Yoritomo''' established '''Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū''' at the site of his grandfather '''Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)''' residence in '''Kyoto''' and dedicated the lands of '''Agawa District, Tosa Province''' as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' ** The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. ** The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' ** Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. ** Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. ** In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. ** The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' ** Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. ** The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. ** Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' [[:en:Tosaden Kōtsū|Tosaden Kōtsū]] Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' ** Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. ** The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. ** Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' [[:en:Tosaden Kōtsū|Tosaden Kōtsū]] Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website]
