revision.text:
* {{Shrine | Name = Wakamiya Hachimangū | location = [[:en:Kōchi, Kōchi|Kōchi, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|29|41.96|N|133|32|37|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} | Deities = {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇}} (Emperor Ōjin)
{{ill|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后}} (Empress Jingū)
{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime, Tagitsuhime, Takiribime)
[[:en:Kuraokami|Ogame-no-Kami]] (enshrined in an auxiliary hall) | Founded = Traditionally '''1185 (Bunji 1), December 30''' | Former Ranking = '''Former Prefectural Shrine (県社)''' | Annual Festival = '''Kanda Matsuri''' (1st Saturday of April, lasting 3 days)
'''Autumn Festival''' (November 6) | Main Hall = '''Three-bay nagare-zukuri (三間社流造)''' | image = [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 06.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan'''. It was historically classified as a '''prefectural shrine (県社)''' under the '''modern shrine Ranking system (近代社格制度)'''. The shrine crest (神紋) is the '''Maridomoe (まり巴)'''. == Deities == * '''Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)''' – [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * '''Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)''' – [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * '''{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}''' – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * '''Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)''' – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the '''Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)''', in '''1185 (Bunji 1)''', after defeating the '''Taira clan''', '''Minamoto no Yoritomo''' established '''Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū''' at the site of his grandfather '''Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)''' residence in '''Kyoto''' and dedicated the lands of '''Agawa District, Tosa Province''' as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' ** The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. ** The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' ** Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. ** Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. ** In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. ** The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' ** Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. ** The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. ** Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' [[:en:Tosaden Kōtsū|Tosaden Kōtsū]] Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡宮 (高知市)|version=103245943|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] }} [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡大神宮]] [[ja:神楽坂若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市十日市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] [[ja:日出若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (加須市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (名古屋市中区)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]] [[sn:akamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Chōsokabe clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kochi, Kochi]] [[Category:History of Kochi, Kochi]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kochi)}}

* {{Shrine | Name = Wakamiya Hachimangū | location = [[:en:Kōchi, Kōchi|Kōchi, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|33|29|41.96|N|133|32|37|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} | Deities = {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇}} (Emperor Ōjin)
{{ill|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后}} (Empress Jingū)
{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime, Tagitsuhime, Takiribime)
[[:en:Kuraokami|Ogame-no-Kami]] (enshrined in an auxiliary hall) | Founded = Traditionally '''1185 (Bunji 1), December 30''' | Former Ranking = '''Former Prefectural Shrine (県社)''' | Annual Festival = '''Kanda Matsuri''' (1st Saturday of April, lasting 3 days)
'''Autumn Festival''' (November 6) | Main Hall = '''Three-bay nagare-zukuri (三間社流造)''' | image = [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 06.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan'''. It was historically classified as a '''prefectural shrine (県社)''' under the '''modern shrine Ranking system (近代社格制度)'''. The shrine crest (神紋) is the '''Maridomoe (まり巴)'''. == Deities == * '''Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)''' – [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * '''Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)''' – [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * '''{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}''' – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * '''Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)''' – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the '''Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)''', in '''1185 (Bunji 1)''', after defeating the '''Taira clan''', '''Minamoto no Yoritomo''' established '''Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū''' at the site of his grandfather '''Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)''' residence in '''Kyoto''' and dedicated the lands of '''Agawa District, Tosa Province''' as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' ** The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. ** The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' ** Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. ** Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. ** In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. ** The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' ** Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. ** The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. ** Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' [[:en:Tosaden Kōtsū|Tosaden Kōtsū]] Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡宮 (高知市)|version=103245943|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] }} [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡大神宮]] [[ja:神楽坂若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市十日市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] [[ja:日出若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (加須市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (名古屋市中区)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]] [[sn:akamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Chōsokabe clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kochi, Kochi]] [[Category:History of Kochi, Kochi]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kochi)}}

At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' ** Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. ** The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. ** Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also Worship Hall }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan'''. It was historically classified as a '''prefectural shrine (県社)''' under the '''modern shrine Ranking system (近代社格制度)'''. The shrine crest (神紋) is the '''Maridomoe (まり巴)'''. == Deities == * '''Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)''' – [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * '''Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)''' – [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * '''{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}''' – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * '''Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)''' – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the '''Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)''', in '''1185 (Bunji 1)''', after defeating the '''Taira clan''', '''Minamoto no Yoritomo''' established '''Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū''' at the site of his grandfather '''Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)''' residence in '''Kyoto''' and dedicated the lands of '''Agawa District, Tosa Province''' as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' ** The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. ** The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' ** Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. ** Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. ** In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. ** The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' ** Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. ** The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. ** Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' [[:en:Tosaden Kōtsū|Tosaden Kōtsū]] Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. ** The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' ** Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. ** Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. ** In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. ** The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' ** Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. ** The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. ** Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' [[:en:Tosaden Kōtsū|Tosaden Kōtsū]] Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] [[:en:Kuraokami|Ogame-no-Kami]] (enshrined in an auxiliary hall) | Founded = Traditionally '''1185 (Bunji 1), December 30''' | Former Ranking = '''Former Prefectural Shrine (県社)''' | Annual Festival = '''Kanda Matsuri''' (1st Saturday of April, lasting 3 days)
'''Autumn Festival''' (November 6) | Main Hall = '''Three-bay nagare-zukuri (三間社流造)''' | image = File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 06.jpg | location = {{ill|Kochi|qid=Q200251}} | deity = {{ill|Ōjin|qid=Q317997}} }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan'''. It was historically classified as a '''prefectural shrine (県社)''' under the '''modern shrine Ranking system (近代社格制度)'''. The shrine crest (神紋) is the '''Maridomoe (まり巴)'''. == Deities == * '''Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)''' – [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * '''Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)''' – [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * '''{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}''' – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * '''Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)''' – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the '''Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)''', in '''1185 (Bunji 1)''', after defeating the '''Taira clan''', '''Minamoto no Yoritomo''' established '''Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū''' at the site of his grandfather '''Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)''' residence in '''Kyoto''' and dedicated the lands of '''Agawa District, Tosa Province''' as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' ** The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. ** The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' ** Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. ** Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. ** In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. ** The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' ** Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. ** The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. ** Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' [[:en:Tosaden Kōtsū|Tosaden Kōtsū]] Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Kōtsū|Tosaden Kōtsū]] Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] (大分市)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]] [[sn:akamiya Hachiman-gū (Kōchi)]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Chōsokabe clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kochi, Kochi]] [[Category:History of {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11616254]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya Hachiman-gū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Wakamiya Hachimangū | location = [[:en:Kōchi, Kōchi|Kōchi, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.495|N|133.54361111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} (Emperor Ōjin)
{{ill|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}} (Empress Jingū)
{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime, Tagitsuhime, Takiribime)
[[:en:Kuraokami|Ogame-no-Kami]] (enshrined in an auxiliary hall) | Founded = Traditionally '''1185 (Bunji 1), December 30''' | Former Ranking = '''Former Prefectural Shrine (県社)''' | Annual Festival = '''Kanda Matsuri''' (1st Saturday of April, lasting 3 days)
'''Autumn Festival''' (November 6) | Main Hall = '''Three-bay nagare-zukuri (三間社流造)''' | image = File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 06.jpg | location = {{ill|Kochi|qid=Q200251}} | deity = {{ill|Ōjin|qid=Q317997}} }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan'''. It was historically classified as a '''prefectural shrine (県社)''' under the '''modern shrine Ranking system (近代社格制度)'''. The shrine crest (神紋) is the '''Maridomoe (まり巴)'''. == Deities == * '''Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)''' – [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * '''Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)''' – [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * '''{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}''' – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * '''Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)''' – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the '''Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)''', in '''1185 (Bunji 1)''', after defeating the '''Taira clan''', '''Minamoto no Yoritomo''' established '''Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū''' at the site of his grandfather '''Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)''' residence in '''Kyoto''' and dedicated the lands of '''Agawa District, Tosa Province''' as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' ** The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. ** The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' ** Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. ** Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. ** In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. ** The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' ** Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. ** The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. ** Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' [[:en:Tosaden Kōtsū|Tosaden Kōtsū]] Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡宮 (高知市)|version=103245943|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] }} [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡大神宮]] [[ja:神楽坂若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市十日市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡宮社]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] [[ja:日出若宮八幡神社]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (加須市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (名古屋市中区)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Chōsokabe clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kochi, Kochi]] [[Category:History of Kochi, Kochi]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kochi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11616254}}

The shrine crest (神紋) is the '''Maridomoe (まり巴)'''. == Deities == * '''Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)''' – [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * '''Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)''' – [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * '''{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}''' – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * '''Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)''' – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the '''Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)''', in '''1185 (Bunji 1)''', after defeating the '''Taira clan''', '''Minamoto no Yoritomo''' established '''Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū''' at the site of his grandfather '''Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)''' residence in '''Kyoto''' and dedicated the lands of '''Agawa District, Tosa Province''' as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' ** The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. ** The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' ** Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. ** Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. ** In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. ** The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' ** Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. ** The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. ** Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Kōtsū|Tosaden Kōtsū]] Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] [[:en:Kuraokami|Ogame-no-Kami]] (enshrined in an auxiliary hall) | Founded = Traditionally '''1185 (Bunji 1), December 30''' | Former Ranking = '''Former Prefectural Shrine (県社)''' | Annual Festival = '''Kanda Matsuri''' (1st Saturday of April, lasting 3 days)
'''Autumn Festival''' (November 6) | Main Hall = '''Three-bay nagare-zukuri (三間社流造)''' | image = File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 06.jpg | location = {{ill|Kochi|qid=Q200251}} | deity = {{ill|Ōjin|qid=Q317997}} }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan'''. It was historically classified as a '''prefectural shrine (県社)''' under the '''modern shrine Ranking system (近代社格制度)'''. The shrine crest (神紋) is the '''Maridomoe (まり巴)'''. == Deities == * '''Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)''' – [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * '''Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)''' – [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * '''{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}''' – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * '''Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)''' – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the '''Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)''', in '''1185 (Bunji 1)''', after defeating the '''Taira clan''', '''Minamoto no Yoritomo''' established '''Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū''' at the site of his grandfather '''Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)''' residence in '''Kyoto''' and dedicated the lands of '''Agawa District, Tosa Province''' as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' ** The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. ** The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' ** Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. ** Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. ** In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. ** The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' ** Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. ** The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. ** Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' [[:en:Tosaden Kōtsū|Tosaden Kōtsū]] Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' ** The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. ** The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' ** Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. ** Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. ** In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. ** The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' ** Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. ** The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. ** Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' [[:en:Tosaden Kōtsū|Tosaden Kōtsū]] Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] [[:en:Kuraokami|Ogame-no-Kami]] (enshrined in an auxiliary hall) | Founded = Traditionally '''1185 (Bunji 1), December 30''' | Former Ranking = '''Former Prefectural Shrine (県社)''' | Annual Festival = '''Kanda Matsuri''' (1st Saturday of April, lasting 3 days)
'''Autumn Festival''' (November 6) | Main Hall = '''Three-bay nagare-zukuri (三間社流造)''' | image = File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 06.jpg | location = {{ill|Kochi|WD=Q200251}} | deity = {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan'''. It was historically classified as a '''prefectural shrine (県社)''' under the '''modern shrine Ranking system (近代社格制度)'''. The shrine crest (神紋) is the '''Maridomoe (まり巴)'''. == Deities == * '''Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)''' – [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * '''Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)''' – [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * '''{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}''' – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * '''Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)''' – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the '''Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)''', in '''1185 (Bunji 1)''', after defeating the '''Taira clan''', '''Minamoto no Yoritomo''' established '''Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū''' at the site of his grandfather '''Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)''' residence in '''Kyoto''' and dedicated the lands of '''Agawa District, Tosa Province''' as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' ** The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. ** Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. ** In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. ** The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' ** Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. ** The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. ** Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' [[:en:Tosaden Kōtsū|Tosaden Kōtsū]] Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] [[sn:commons:Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Kochi)]][[ja:若宮八幡宮 (高知市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11616254]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya Hachiman-gū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Wakamiya Hachimangū | location = [[:en:Kōchi, Kōchi|Kōchi, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.495|N|133.54361111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} (Emperor Ōjin)
{{ill|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}} (Empress Jingū)
{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime, Tagitsuhime, Takiribime)
[[:en:Kuraokami|Ogame-no-Kami]] (enshrined in an auxiliary hall) | Founded = Traditionally '''1185 (Bunji 1), December 30''' | Former Ranking = '''Former Prefectural Shrine (県社)''' | Annual Festival = '''Kanda Matsuri''' (1st Saturday of April, lasting 3 days)
'''Autumn Festival''' (November 6) | Main Hall = '''Three-bay nagare-zukuri (三間社流造)''' | image = File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 06.jpg | location = {{ill|Kochi|WD=Q200251}} | deity = {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Kōchi City, Kōchi Prefecture, Japan'''. It was historically classified as a '''prefectural shrine (県社)''' under the '''modern shrine Ranking system (近代社格制度)'''. The shrine crest (神紋) is the '''Maridomoe (まり巴)'''. == Deities == * '''Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)''' – [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * '''Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)''' – [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * '''{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}''' – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * '''Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)''' – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the '''Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)''', in '''1185 (Bunji 1)''', after defeating the '''Taira clan''', '''Minamoto no Yoritomo''' established '''Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū''' at the site of his grandfather '''Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)''' residence in '''Kyoto''' and dedicated the lands of '''Agawa District, Tosa Province''' as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' ** The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. ** The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' ** Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. ** Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. ** In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. ** The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' ** Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. ** The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. ** Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. === Goshinkōsai (御神幸祭) === Known as '''"Onabare" (おなばれ)''', a traditional '''procession featuring mikoshi''' and shrine followers in '''historical attire'''. == Access == * '''Bus:''' [[:en:Tosaden Kōtsū|Tosaden Kōtsū]] Katsurahama Line – '''5-minute walk from Nankai Chūgakkō-mae bus stop.''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://wakamiya-kochi.com/ Official Website] (大分市)]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Chōsokabe clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kochi, Kochi]] [[Category:History of Kochi, Kochi]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kōchi The shrine crest (神紋) is the '''Maridomoe (まり巴)'''. == Deities == * '''Hondawake-no-Mikoto (誉田別命)''' – [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * '''Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto (息長帯比売命)''' – [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * '''{{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}''' – {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Takiribime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * '''Ogame-no-Kami (淤加美神)''' – Enshrined in an auxiliary shrine == History == According to the '''Azuma Kagami (吾妻鏡)''', in '''1185 (Bunji 1)''', after defeating the '''Taira clan''', '''Minamoto no Yoritomo''' established '''Rokujō Wakamiya Hachimangū''' at the site of his grandfather '''Minamoto no Tameyoshi's (源為義)''' residence in '''Kyoto''' and dedicated the lands of '''Agawa District, Tosa Province''' as the shrine’s domain. At the same time, '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' was enshrined in this location for the protection of the shrine estate. During the '''Sengoku period''', in '''1560 (Eiroku 3)''', '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' established his battle camp at Wakamiya Hachimangū’s horse grounds during his '''first battle''', which ended in victory. From then on, he '''prayed for victory at the shrine before every campaign'''. Under the '''Yamauchi clan''', who became '''daimyō of Tosa Domain''' during the '''Edo period''', the shrine continued to receive strong patronage and was designated as one of the domain's '''eight official prayer shrines'''. During the '''Meiji period''', with the introduction of the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', the shrine was classified as a '''village shrine (郷社) in 1873 (Meiji 6)''', and later elevated to '''prefectural shrine (県社) in 1930 (Shōwa 5).''' Today, it serves as the '''main shrine for the southern Kōchi City areas''', including '''Nagahama, Mitomezaki, Urado, and Yokohama'''. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 05.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Torii marking the site where Chōsokabe Motochika destroyed the original torii]] [[File:Chosokabe.jpg|thumb|200px|Statue of Chōsokabe Motochika's first battle]] * '''Worship Hall (拝殿)''' ** The hall was rebuilt in a '''"de-tonbō-zukuri" (出蜻蛉式)''' style by '''Chōsokabe Motochika''' to ensure victory in battle. In contrast, the '''Tosa Shrine (土佐神社) worship hall''' was designed in '''"iri-tonbō-zukuri" (入蜻蛉式)''' style for victory reporting. ** The '''tonbo (蜻蛉, dragonfly)''' is known as the '''"winning insect"''' (勝ち虫) in Japanese culture, symbolizing victory. * '''Torii (鳥居)''' ** Before departing for the '''Kyūshū campaign against the Shimazu clan in 1586 (Tenshō 14)''', Motochika prayed for victory at the shrine. However, his '''battle standard became caught on the torii''', which some viewed as an '''ill omen'''. ** Motochika dismissed these concerns, interpreting the event as a '''good sign''' that they would '''conquer the enemy''', but suffered '''a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hetsugigawa''', losing his '''heir, Chōsokabe Nobuchika'''. ** In anger, he '''destroyed the torii and cast it into the sea'''. The torii's '''foundation resurfaced''' in a '''1865 (Keiō 1) earthquake''', leading to its reconstruction in '''1870 (Meiji 3)'''. ** The current '''reinforced concrete torii''' was built in '''1980 (Shōwa 55)'''. * '''Chōsokabe Motochika First Battle Statue (長宗我部元親初陣像)''' ** Unveiled in '''1999 (Heisei 11)''' for the '''400th anniversary of Motochika’s death'''. ** The statue depicts Motochika gripping a '''spear''' and attempting to seize a '''map of Shikoku''', which displays the region's '''territorial divisions at the time of his first battle'''. ** Height: '''~7 meters (including base)''', Spear length: '''~6 meters'''. == Auxiliary Shrines (摂末社) == * '''Tenjin Shrine (天神宮)''' – '''Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami (天水分大神)''', or possibly '''Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane)''' * '''Kōjin Shrine (荒神宮, Ōkōjin)''' – '''Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto (大年御祖神), Ōtsuchi-no-Mikoto (大土御祖神)''' * '''Sanza Shrine (三座神社)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (山祇大神), Ukanomitama-no-Kami (宇迦之御魂神), Ōkuninushi-no-Kami (大国主神)''' * '''Sansha Shrine (三社神社, Tsubaki Shrine/Yakujin Shrine)''' – '''Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神), Mizuhanome-no-Kami (水波能売神), Sukuna-Hikona-no-Kami (少彦名神)''' * '''Hayato Shrine (隼人社)''' – '''Fukudome Hayato-no-Kami (福留隼人正)''' == Major Festivals == === Kanda Matsuri (神田祭) === [[File:Kochi Yakamiya Hachimangu 08.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sacred rice field for Kanda Matsuri]] Also known as the '''"Doronkō Matsuri" (どろんこ祭り, Mud Festival)''', this '''rice planting festival''' involves young women (早乙女) covering men in '''mud''', signifying '''fertility and health'''. ===
